expectations.text: Output identifies DATE(l_shipdate) as redundant/non-sargable and explains that l_shipdate is already a DATE type
expectations.evidence: Problem Analysis: 'l_shipdate is already a date type column. The DATE() function... does nothing useful... prevents index usage.'

expectations.text: Rewritten SQL removes DATE() from SELECT, WHERE, and GROUP BY clauses
expectations.evidence: Optimized Query: SELECT l_shipdate ... WHERE l_shipdate >= ... AND l_shipdate < ... GROUP BY l_shipdate ORDER BY l_shipdate

expectations.text: Rewritten SQL converts BETWEEN to explicit range conditions or maintains sargable equivalents
expectations.evidence: WHERE uses >= AND < range instead of BETWEEN

expectations.text: Plan comparison shows expected elimination of full table scan (type changes from ALL to range)
expectations.evidence: Plan Comparison: scan type changes from ALL (full table scan) to range, rows from 6M to ~240K

expectations.text: Output includes or considers an index recommendation (covering index or composite index)
expectations.evidence: Additional Optimization section: CREATE INDEX idx_lineitem_shipdate_covering ON lineitem (l_shipdate, l_quantity)
